title: S/R detection engine (proximity trigger → signal emission)
acceptance_criteria: Engine consumes the Redis straddle.values stream (its own consumer group, e.g. 'sr-detection') and maintains per-underlying state (mirroring peak-detection-engine's UnderlyingState pattern) so a single process handles all active underlyings.

Loads the day's S/R levels from src/signals/sr-levels.ts (T-43-B); if the freshness guard throws for an underlying, S/R is disabled for that underlying for the session and logged loudly — no signals emitted for it.

When spot comes within sr_proximity_points of a strong level (strength >= configured floor), emits an SR signal: writes straddle_signals with sr_subtype='SR_REVERSAL', sr_strength, poc_used, and level_source JSON (R-E), populating spot/atm_strike/straddle_value/vix from the snapshot; then publishes to signals.generated.
VIX-null handling: neutral weight, never divides by/assumes a VIX value (R-I).
Emission is gated on ACTIVE_PHASE>=2 (read from env) so no SR rows pollute Phase-1 data (R-F).
De-duplication window prevents repeated emission for the same level within a configurable interval (baseline dedup; full hysteresis is optional rec R1, not in scope here).

Unit tests: proximity trigger, strength floor, poc_used/level_source tagging, VIX-null path, ACTIVE_PHASE gate (no emission when <2), freshness-disable path, dedup window. tsc --noEmit clean; injectable Clock; named exports.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision (esp. proximity/strength thresholds, dedup window, and consumer-group naming)
